title: 第 57 節
en: 5. Therefore they call even now a man who does not give alms here, who has no faith, and offers no sacrifices, an Asura, for this is the doctrine (upanishad) of the Asuras. They deck out the body of the dead with perfumes, flowers, and fine raiment by way of ornament, and think they will thus conquer that world l.
Ninth Khanda.

1. But Indra, before he had returned to the Devas, saw this difficulty. As this self (the shadow in the water)2 is well adorned, when the body is well adorned, well dressed, when the body is well dressed, well cleaned, if the body is wTell cleaned, that self will also be blind, if the body is blind, lame, if the body is lame 3, crippled, if the body is crippled, and will perish in fact as soon as the body perishes. Therefore I see no good in this (doctrine).

2. Taking fuel in his hand he came a^ain as a pupil to Pra^apati. Pra^apati said to him : ' Maghavat (Indra), as you went away with Viro/£ana, satisfied in your heart, for wrhat purpose did you come back ? '
1 This evidently refers to the customs and teaching of tribes not entirely conforming to the Brahmanic system. Whether the adorning of the dead body implies burial instead of burning, seems doubtful.

2 The commentator remarks that though both Indra and ViroX'ana had mistaken the true import of what Pra^apati said, yet while Viro/£ana took the body to be the Self, Indra thought that the Self was the shadow of the body.
3 Srama, lame, is explained by the commentator as one-eyed, ekanetra.
I38 tftfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.

He said : ' Sir, as this self (the shadow) is well adorned, when the body is well adorned, well dressed, when the body is well dressed, well cleaned, if the body is well cleaned, that self will also be blind, if the body is blind, lame, if the body is lame, crippled, if the body is crippled, and will perish in fact as soon as the body perishes. Therefore I see no good in this (doctrine).'
3. ' So it is indeed, Maghavat,' replied Pra^apati ; 'but I shall explain him (the true Self) further to you. Live with me another thirty-two years.'
He lived with him another thirty-two years, and then Pra^apati said :
Tenth Khanda.
1. 'He who moves about happy in dreams, he is the Self, this is the immortal, the fearless, this is Brahman.'

Then Indra went away satisfied in his heart. But before he had returned to the Devas, he saw this difficulty. Although it is true that that self is not blind, even if the body is blind, nor lame, if the body is lame, though it is true that that self is not rendered faulty by the faults of it (the body), 2. Nor struck when it (the body) is struck, nor lamed when it is lamed, yet it is as if they struck him (the self) in dreams, as if they chased him \ 1 I have adopted the reading vi/£X'Myayanti, because it is the most difficult, and therefore explains most easily the various corruptions, or it may be emendations, that have crept into the text. -Sahkara explains vi/WMdayanti by vidravayanti, and this shows that he too must have read xikkMya.ya.nti, for he could not have explained viMMdayanti, which means they uncover or they deprive of their clothing, by vidravayanti, they drive away. It is true that viX'^ayayanti may be explained in two ways ; it may be the causative of khz, to cut, but this meaning is not very appropriate here, VIII PRAPA77/AKA, IO KHANDA, 4. 139 He becomes even conscious, as it were, of pain, and sheds tears. Therefore I see no good in this.
3. Taking fuel in his hands, he went again as a pupil to Pra^apati. Pra^apati said to him : ' Maghavat, as you went away satisfied in your heart, for what purpose did you come back ? '

He said : ' Sir, although it is true that that self is not blind even if the body is blind, nor lame, if the body is lame, though it is true that that self is not rendered faulty by the faults of it (the body), 4. Nor struck when it (the body) is struck, nor lamed when it is lamed, yet it is as if they struck him (the self) in dreams, as if they chased him. He becomes even conscious, as it were, of pain, and sheds tears. Therefore I see no good in this.'
' So it is indeed, Maghavat,' replied Pra^apati ; ' but I shall explain him (the true Self) further to you. Live with me another thirty-two years.'
He lived with him another thirty-two years. Then Pra^apati said :

and quite inadmissible in another passage where vL£/£Myayati occurs, whereas, if derived from x'xkh (oi^o/xat) in a causative sense, Sahkara could hardly have chosen a better explanation than vidravayanti, they make run away. The root \\kh, vi/WMyayati is recognised in Pacini III, 1, 28, and in the Dhatupa//za 28, 129, but it has hitherto been met with in this passage only, and in Brz'hadara«yaka Up. IV, 3, 20. Here also the author speaks of a man who imagines that people kill him or do him violence, or that an elephant chases him or that he falls into a pit. Here we have hastiva vi£/£Myayati, and -Sahkara, at least as printed by Dr. Roer, explains this by vi/£/Wapayati, vi£/£Mdayati, vidravayati ; dhavatity artha//. Much better is Dvivedaganga's commentary, as published by Dr. Weber, -Satap. Brahm. p. 1145, KadaX-id enazrc hasti vi/WMyayativa vidravayativa ; vi/Wa gatau, gupudhupavi£/ripawipanibhya aya iti (Pa«. Ill, 1,28) svartha ayapratyaya.^. In the Dictionary of Boehtlingk and Roth the derivation from khi, to cut, is preferred; see Nachtrage, s.v. kM.
1 40 a'fandogya-upanishad.
Eleventh Khan da.
1. 'When a man being asleep, reposing, and at perfect rest 1, sees no dreams, that is the Self, this is the immortal, the fearless, this is Brahman.'

Then Indra went away satisfied in his heart. But before he had returned to the Devas, he saw this difficulty. In truth he thus does not know himself (his self) that he is I, nor does he know anything that exists. He is gone to utter annihilation. I see no eood in this.
2. Taking fuel in his hand he went again as a pupil to Pra^apati. Pra^apati said to him : ' Maghavat, as you went away satisfied in your heart, for what purpose did you come back ? '
He said : ' Sir, in that way he does not know himself (his self) that he is I, nor does he know anything that exists. He is gone to utter annihilation. I see no orood in this.'
3. ' So it is indeed, Maghavat,' replied Pra^apati ; ' but I shall explain him (the true Self) further to you, and nothing more than this 2. Live here other five years.'

He lived there other five years. This made in all one hundred and one years, and therefore it is said that Indra Maghavat lived one hundred and one years as a pupil with Pra^apati. Pra^apati said to him :
Twelfth Khaa7#a.
1 . ' Maghavat, this body is mortal and always held by death. It is the abode of that Self which is 1 See Kh. Up. VIII, 6, 3.
2 -Sankara explains this as meaning the real Self, not anything different from the Self.
zh: 因此，他們即使到了今日，仍然稱呼那些不施捨、無信仰、不獻祭的人為阿修羅（Asura），因為這乃是阿修羅的教義（upanishad）。他們用香油、花卉與華美的衣裳來裝飾死者的遺體作為裝飾，自以為如此便能征服來世 l。
第九明分
但因陀羅在回到提婆之前，看到了這個困難。因為這個自我（水中的影子）2當身體裝飾得很好時，就裝飾得很好；當身體穿著得很好時，就穿著得很好；當身體清潔得很好時，如果身體清潔得很好，那麼這個自我也會盲目，如果身體盲目的話，會跛足，如果身體跛足3、殘廢，如果身體殘廢，而且實際上會在身體死亡時立即滅亡。因此我看不出這個（學說）有什麼好處。
2. 他手持柴薪再度作為弟子來到梵天（Prajāpati）面前。梵天對他說：「摩訶婆多（因陀羅），你既然與毗樓波那（Virochana）一起離去，心中滿足，如今為何又回來了？」
這顯然涉及不完全符合婆羅門教體系的部落的習俗與教導。死者遺體的裝飾是否意味著土葬而非火葬，似乎還有疑問。
評論者指出，雖然因陀羅（Indra）和毗羅遮那（Viro/£ana）都誤解了梵天（Prajāpati）所言的真正含義，但毗羅遮那將身體視為自我（Self），而因陀羅則認為自我乃是身體的影子。
3 沙摩（Srama）一詞，解作「瘸」（lame），而注釋者將其解釋為獨眼（ekanetra）。

我注意到您提供的「英文原文」(I38 tftfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD) 看起來並非清晰的英文文本，而是包含亂碼字符（tftf）和奧義書梵文名稱的混合。 這似乎是原始文檔的掃描或轉錄錯誤。為了提供準確的翻譯，我需要： **請您提供清晰的英文原文段落**，以便我進行專業的繁體中文翻譯。 如果您是想翻譯與《歌者奧義書》(Chandogya-Upanishad) 相關的穆勒著作段落，敬請貼上完整清晰的英文原文。
他說：「先生，既然這個自我（影子）是依附於身體而得以妝扮的，當身體妝扮得好時，自我也就妝扮得好；當身體穿著得當時，自我也就穿著得當；當身體潔淨時，自我也就潔淨。然而，如果身體瞎眼，自我也將瞎眼；如果身體跛足，自我也將跛足；如果身體殘廢，自我也將殘廢；並且自我將隨著身體的消亡而實際上立即消亡。因此，我看不出這（學說）有何益處。」
3. 「確實如此，摩笈婆多（Maghavat）」跋梨波提（Pra^apati）答道；「但我將進一步為你解釋他（真實的自我）。再與我一起生活三十二年。」
他與他一起生活了另外三十二年，之後普拉阿帕蒂（Pra^apati）說：
第十篇章
「那在夢中快樂地遊行者，他就是自我（Self），這是不朽者，無所畏懼者，這就是梵天（Brahman）。」

然後因陀羅心滿意足地離開了。但在他回到提婆神之前，他看到了這個難題。雖然確實那個自我並非盲目的，即使身體是盲的，也不會跛足，若身體跛足的話，雖然確實那個自我不會因它（身體）的缺陷而受損，2. 當它（身體）遭受打擊時亦不被打擊，當它跛足時亦不跛足，然而卻彷彿在夢中有人打擊他（自我），彷彿有人追趕他\ 1 我採納了 vi/£X'Myayanti 這個讀法，因為它最為艱難，因此最能輕易解釋已竄入本文的各種訛誤或修正。商迦羅將 vi/WMdayanti 解釋為 vidravayanti，這表明他也必定讀過 xikkMya.ya.nti，因為他無法將意為「揭露」或「脫去衣著」的 viMMdayanti 解釋為 vidravayanti（意為「驅趕」）。誠然 viX'^ayayanti 可有兩種解法；它可能是 khz（切割）的使役態，但這個意義在此並不十分恰當，VIII PRAPA77/AKA, IO KHANDA, 4. 139 他變得彷彿甚至意識到了痛苦，並流下淚水。因此我認為這沒有任何益處。
3. 他手中拿著燃料，再次作為弟子到訪普拉賈帕提。普拉賈帕提對他說：「馬格哈瓦特，既然你滿心歡喜而去，你為何又回來了呢？」
他說：「先生，雖然確實那個自我既不因身體的盲瞎而盲瞎，也不因身體的跛足而跛足，雖然確實那個自我不因身體的缺陷而有所缺陷，4. 也不因身體被擊中而被擊中，也不因身體跛足而跛足，然而就好像他們在夢中擊中他（那個自我）一樣，就好像他們追趕他。他就像是變得有所覺知，感受到痛苦，並流下眼淚。因此我看不出這樣有什麼好處。」
「正是如此，摩奴之主啊，」梵天答道：「但我將進一步為你解釋他（真我）。與我再住三十二年。」
他與他同住了另外三十二年。然後跋梨波提（Prajāpati）說：

而在另一處出現 vL£/£Myayati 的地方則完全不可接受；但若從 x'xkh（oi^o/xat）推導，取因果義，Sahkara 幾乎不可能選到比 vidravayanti（他們使之奔逃）更恰當的解釋了。語根 \\kh, vi/WMyayati 見於《帕尼尼語法》（Pacini）III, 1, 28，以及《根詞辭典》（Dhatupa//za）28, 129，但迄今僅在此段落及《廣林奧義書》（Brz'hadara«yaka Up.）IV, 3, 20 中出現過。此處作者亦談及一個人想像人們殺害他或對他施暴，或大象追逐他，或他墜入坑中的情形。在此我們見有 hastiva vi£/£Myayati，而 Sahkara（至少按 Roer 博士的印本）解釋為 vi/£/Wapayati, vi£/£Mdayati, vidravayati；dhavatity artha//。Dvivedaganga 的註疏更為優越，如 Weber 博士所發行的版本，《百路梵書》（Satap. Brahm.）第 1145 頁所載：KadaX-id enazrc hasti vi/WMyayativa vidravayativa；vi/Wa gatau, gupudhupavi£/ripawipanibhya aya iti（Pa«. Ill, 1,28）svartha ayapratyaya。在 Boehtlingk 與 Roth 的《字典》中，更傾向於從 khi（切割）推導；見《補編》（Nachtrage），s.v. kM。
阿坦陀奧義書（Chāndogya-upanishad）
第十一個卡罕達（Eleventh Khan da）。
「當一個人處於睡眠、安息與完全靜止的狀態，不見任何夢象時，那就是自我，這就是不朽者、無畏者，這就是梵天。」¹
於是因陀羅心滿意足地離開了。然而在他回到眾提婆之前，他看到了這個困難。實則他並不認識他自己（他的自我）即他就是我，也不認識任何存在之物。他已陷入徹底的虛無。我看不出這有何益處。
2. 他手持燃料再次作為弟子來到梵天主（Prajāpati）面前。梵天主對他說：「馬格瓦特，既然你滿心歡喜地離去，你為了什麼目的又回來了？」
他說：「先生，這樣一來，他就不認識自己（他的自我）即『我』，也不認識任何存在之物。他已步入完全的虛無。我看不出這有什麼善處。」
3. 『誠然如此，摩迦婆特，』普拉闍巴蒂回答說；『但我將進一步為你闡釋他（真實的自我），除此之外別無他物。活在此地再五年吧。』
他在那裡又住了五年。這樣加起來共一百零一年，因此據說因陀羅‧摩羯婆陀（Indra Maghavat）作為跋羅訶波提（Pra^apati）的弟子生活了一百零一年。跋羅訶波提對他說：
第十二講（Khaa7#a）
『摩訶婆多，此身體乃是必死之物，常為死亡所執。它是那個自我的住所』¹ ¹ 見《歌者奧義書》第八卷，第六章，第三節。
商羯羅（Sankara）將此詮釋為真實的自我，而非有別於自我之物。
